nameMeanings.name: Silverside
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English silver ‘silver’ + side ‘side (of the body, ?head)’. Compare Richard Silvereghe (‘silver eye’), 1414–15 in Inquisitiones post Mortem (Yorks); Adam Siluermouth (‘silver mouth’), 1379 in Yorks Poll Tax ; John Silvertop (‘silver hair’), 1478 in York Freemen’s Register . Some early bearers may belong with sense 2.
2  English: locative name from Silver Side in Farlam (Cumb), so recorded in 1485.

variants.name: Yorks Poll Tax
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name May + the patronymic marker -son.
2 English: a northern spelling of Mason . Compare Adam de Morton, mayson, 1371 in Yorks Poll Tax .

variants.name: York Freemen’s Register
meanings: English: (i) occupational name from northern Middle English * gater ‘goatherd’, a derivative of Middle English gayte ‘goat’ (from Old English gāt, with preservation of the unrounded northern ā, or from Old Scandinavian geit). See also Goater . The name is found in N England and the Midlands, and has probably also absorbed Middle English gaythird ‘goatherd’, as in John le Gaythirde, 1301 in Subsidy Rolls (Yorks), Robert Gayterd, 1466 in York Freemen’s Register . (ii) locative name from Middle English * gater ‘dweller by the town gate or gateway’, see Gate . This type of name, derived from a topographical term + -er, is mainly found in S England, especially Kent, Sussex, and Hants.

variants.name: Inquisitiones post Mortem
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English seli ‘happy, blessed’ (Old English sǣlig) + infaunt(e) ‘child’ (Old French enfant). Compare William Selisaule, 1268 in Inquisitiones post Mortem (Yorks) ‘fortunate soul, person’, and see Silabon .

variants.name: Silversides
meanings: English: variant of Silverside with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Silverside II was born in 1520, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, his father, Thomas Silverside, was 20 and his mother, Emma Elizabeth Grey, was 17. He had at least 1 daughter with Janet Grey. He died on 19 December 1604, in Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Silverside II est né en 1520, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Silverside, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Emma Elizabeth Grey, avait 17 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Janet Grey. Il est décédé le 19 décembre 1604, à Cirencester, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans.

Er hatte mit Janet Grey mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 19. Dezember 1604 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Silverside II è nato il nel 1520 nel Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Silverside, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Emma Elizabeth Grey, aveva 17 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Janet Grey. Morì il 19 dicembre 1604, nel Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 84 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Silverside II(이)가 1520년 영국 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Silverside(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma Elizabeth Grey(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Janet Grey(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1604년 12월 19일 영국 그루스스터실르 시렌스스터에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.
